book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
Procatechesis, or Prologue to the Catechetical Lectures of our Holy Father, Cyril, Archbishop of Jerusalem.

ccel.title: NPNF2-07. Cyril of Jerusalem, Gregory Nazianzen

source.title: NPNF2-07. Cyril of Jerusalem, Gregory Nazianzen

title: Procatechesis, or Prologue to the Catechetical Lectures of our Holy Father, Cyril, Archbishop of Jerusalem.

source.title: NPNF2-07. Cyril of Jerusalem, Gregory Nazianzen

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Catechetical Lectures

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Archbishop of Jerusalem.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: PROLOGUE TO THE CATECHETICAL LECTURES OF OUR HOLY FATHER,

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: CYRIL, ARCHBISHOP OF JERUSALEM.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Already there is an odour of blessedness upon you, O ye who are soon to be enlightened: already ye are gathering the spiritual flowers, to weave heavenly crowns: already the fragrance of the Holy Spirit has breathed upon you: already ye have gathered round the vestibule of the King’s palace; may ye be led in also by the King!

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For blossoms now have appeared upon the trees; may the fruit also be found perfect!

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thus far there has been an inscription of your names, and a call to service, and torches of the bridal train, and a longing for heavenly citizenship, and a good purpose, and hope attendant thereon.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For he lieth not who said, that to them that love God all things work together for good.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: God is lavish in beneficence, yet He waits for each man’s genuine will: therefore the Apostle added and said, to them that are called according to a purpose.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The honesty of purpose makes thee called: for if thy body be here but not thy mind, it profiteth thee nothing.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Even Simon Magus once came to the Laver: he was baptized, but was not enlightened; and though he dipped his body in water, he enlightened not his heart with the Spirit: his body went down and came up, but his soul was not buried with Christ, nor raised with Him.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Now I mention the statements of (men’s) falls, that thou mayest not fall: for these things happened to them by way of example, and they are written for the admonition of those who to this day draw near.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Let none of you be found tempting His grace, lest any root of bitterness spring up and trouble you.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Let none of you enter saying, Let us see what the faithful are doing: let me go in and see, that I may learn what is being done.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Dost thou expect to see, and not expect to be seen?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And thinkest thou, that whilst thou art searching out what is going on, God is not searching thy heart?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: A certain man in the Gospels once pried into the marriage feast, and took an unbecoming garment, and came in, sat down, and ate: for the bridegroom permitted it.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But when he saw them all clad in white, he ought to have assumed a garment of the same kind himself: whereas he partook of the like food, but was unlike them in fashion and in purpose.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The bridegroom, however, though bountiful, was not undiscerning: and in going round to each of the guests and observing them (for his care was not for their eating, but for their seemly behaviour), he saw a stranger not having on a wedding garment, and said to him, Friend, how camest thou in hither?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: In what a colour!

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: With what a conscience!

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: What though the door-keeper forbade thee not, because of the bountifulness of the entertainer? what though thou wert ignorant in what fashion thou shouldest come in to the banquet?—thou didst come in, and didst see the glittering fashions of the guests: shouldest thou not have been taught even by what was before thine eyes?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Shouldest thou not have retired in good season, that thou mightest enter in good season again?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But now thou hast come in unseasonably, to be unseasonably cast out.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: So he commands the servants, Bind his feet, which daringly intruded: bind his hands, which knew not how to put a bright garment around him: and cast him into the outer darkness; for he is unworthy of the wedding torches.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou seest what happened to that man: make thine own condition safe.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For we, the ministers of Christ, have admitted every one, and occupying, as it were, the place of door-keepers we left the door open: and possibly thou didst enter with thy soul bemired with sins, and with a will defiled.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Enter thou didst, and wast allowed: thy name was inscribed.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Tell me, dost thou behold this venerable constitution of the Church?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Dost thou view her order and discipline, the reading of Scriptures, the presence of the ordained, the course of instruction?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Be abashed at the place, and be taught by what thou seest.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Go out opportunely now, and enter most opportunely to-morrow.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: If the fashion of thy soul is avarice, put on another fashion and come in.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Put off thy former fashion, cloke it not up.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Put off, I pray thee, fornication and uncleanness, and put on the brightest robe of chastity.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: This charge I give thee, before Jesus the Bridegroom of souls come in and see their fashions.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: A long notice is allowed thee; thou hast forty days for repentance: thou hast full opportunity both to put off, and wash, and to put on and enter.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But if thou persist in an evil purpose, the speaker is blameless, but thou must not look for the grace: for the water will receive, but the Spirit will not accept thee.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: If any one is conscious of his wound, let him take the salve; if any has fallen, let him arise.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Let there be no Simon among you, no hypocrisy, no idle curiosity about the matter.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Possibly too thou art come on another pretext.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: It is possible that a man is wishing to pay court to a woman, and came hither on that account.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The remark applies in like manner to women also in their turn.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: A slave also perhaps wishes to please his master, and a friend his friend.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: I accept this bait for the hook, and welcome thee, though thou camest with an evil purpose, yet as one to be saved by a good hope.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Perhaps thou knewest not whither thou wert coming, nor in what kind of net thou art taken.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou art come within the Church’s nets: be taken alive, flee not: for Jesus is angling for thee, not in order to kill, but by killing to make alive: for thou must die and rise again.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For thou hast heard the Apostle say, Dead indeed unto sin, but living unto righteousness.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Die to thy sins, and live to righteousness, live from this very day.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: See, I pray thee, how great a dignity Jesus bestows on thee.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou wert called a Catechumen, while the word echoed round thee from without; hearing of hope, and knowing it not; hearing mysteries, and not understanding them; hearing Scriptures, and not knowing their depth.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The echo is no longer around thee, but within thee; for the indwelling Spirit henceforth makes thy mind a house of God.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: When thou shalt have heard what is written concerning the mysteries, then wilt thou understand things which thou knewest not.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And think not that thou receivest a small thing: though a miserable man, thou receivest one of God’s titles.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Hear St. Paul saying, God is faithful.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Hear another Scripture saying, God is faithful and just.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Foreseeing this, the Psalmist, because men are to receive a title of God, spoke thus in the person of God: I said, Ye are Gods, and are all sons of the Most High.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But beware lest thou have the title of “faithful,” but the will of the faithless.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou hast entered into a contest, toil on through the race: another such opportunity thou canst not have.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Were it thy wedding-day before thee, wouldest thou not have disregarded all else, and set about the preparation for the feast?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And on the eve of consecrating thy soul to the heavenly Bridegroom, wilt thou not cease from carnal things, that thou mayest win spiritual?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: We may not receive Baptism twice or thrice; else it might be said, Though I have failed once, I shall set it right a second time: whereas if thou fail once, the thing cannot be set right; for there is one Lord, and one faith, and one baptism: for only the heretics are re-baptized, because the former was no baptism.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For God seeks nothing else from us, save a good purpose.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Say not, How are my sins blotted out?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: I tell thee, By willing, by believing.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: What can be shorter than this?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But if, while thy lips declare thee willing, thy heart be silent, He knoweth the heart, who judgeth thee.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Cease from this day from every evil deed.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Let not thy tongue speak unseemly words, let thine eye abstain from sin, and from roving after things unprofitable.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Let thy feet hasten to the catechisings; receive with earnestness the exorcisms: whether thou be breathed upon or exorcised, the act is to thee salvation.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Suppose thou hast gold unwrought and alloyed, mixed with various substances, copper, and tin, and iron, and lead: we seek to have the gold alone; can gold be purified from the foreign substances without fire?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Even so without exorcisms the soul cannot be purified; and these exorcisms are divine, having been collected out of the divine Scriptures.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thy face has been veiled, that thy mind may henceforward be free, lest the eye by roving make the heart rove also.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But when thine eyes are veiled, thine ears are not hindered from receiving the means of salvation.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For in like manner as those who are skilled in the goldsmith’s craft throw in their breath upon the fire through certain delicate instruments, and blowing up the gold which is hidden in the crucible stir the flame which surrounds it, and so find what they are seeking; even so when the exorcists inspire terror by the Spirit of God, and set the soul, as it were, on fire in the crucible of the body, the hostile demon flees away, and there abide salvation and the hope of eternal life, and the soul henceforth is cleansed from its sins and hath salvation.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Let us then, brethren, abide in hope, and surrender ourselves, and hope, in order that the God of all may see our purpose, and cleanse us from our sins, and impart to us good hopes of our estate, and grant us repentance that bringeth salvation.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: God hath called, and His call is to thee.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Attend closely to the catechisings, and though we should prolong our discourse, let not thy mind be wearied out.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For thou art receiving armour against the adverse power, armour against heresies, against Jews, and Samaritans, and Gentiles.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou hast many enemies; take to thee many darts, for thou hast many to hurl them at: and thou hast need to learn how to strike down the Greek, how to contend against heretic, against Jew and Samaritan.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And the armour is ready, and most ready the sword of the Spirit: but thou also must stretch forth thy right hand with good resolution, that thou mayest war the Lord’s warfare, and overcome adverse powers, and become invincible against every heretical attempt.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Let me give thee this charge also.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Study our teachings and keep them for ever.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Think not that they are the ordinary homilies; for though they also are good and trustworthy, yet if we should neglect them to-day we may study them to-morrow.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But if the teaching concerning the laver of regeneration delivered in a consecutive course be neglected to-day, when shall it be made right?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Suppose it is the season for planting trees: if we do not dig, and dig deep, when else can that be planted rightly which has once been planted ill?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Suppose, pray, that the Catechising is a kind of building: if we do not bind the house together by regular bonds in the building, lest some gap be found, and the building become unsound, even our former labour is of no use.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But stone must follow stone by course, and corner match with corner, and by our smoothing off inequalities the building must thus rise evenly.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: In like manner we are bringing to thee stones, as it were, of knowledge.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou must hear concerning the living God, thou must hear of Judgment, must hear of Christ, and of the Resurrection.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And many things there are to be discussed in succession, which though now dropped one by one are afterwards to be presented in harmonious connexion.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But unless thou fit them together in the one whole, and remember what is first, and what is second, the builder may build, but thou wilt find the building unsound.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: When, therefore, the Lecture is delivered, if a Catechumen ask thee what the teachers have said, tell nothing to him that is without.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For we deliver to thee a mystery, and a hope of the life to come.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Guard the mystery for Him who gives the reward.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Let none ever say to thee, What harm to thee, if I also know it?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: So too the sick ask for wine; but if it be given at a wrong time it causes delirium, and two evils arise; the sick man dies, and the physician is blamed.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thus is it also with the Catechumen, if he hear anything from the believer: both the Catechumen becomes delirious (for he understands not what he has heard, and finds fault with the thing, and scoffs at what is said), and the believer is condemned as a traitor.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But thou art now standing on the border: take heed, pray, to tell nothing out; not that the things spoken are not worthy to be told, but because his ear is unworthy to receive.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou wast once thyself a Catechumen, and I described not what lay before thee.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: When by experience thou hast learned how high are the matters of our teaching, then thou wilt know that the Catechumens are not worthy to hear them.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Ye who have been enrolled are become sons and daughters of one Mother.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: When ye have come in before the hour of the exorcisms, let each one of you speak things tending to godliness: and if any of your number be not present, seek for him.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: If thou wert called to a banquet, wouldest thou not wait for thy fellow guest?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: If thou hadst a brother, wouldest thou not seek thy brother’s good?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Afterwards busy not thyself about unprofitable matters: neither, what the city has done, nor the village, nor the King, nor the Bishop, nor the Presbyter.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Look upward; that is what thy present hour needeth.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Be still, and know that I am God.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: If thou seest the believers ministering, and shewing no care, they enjoy security, they know what they have received, they are in possession of grace.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But thou standest just now in the turn of the scale, to be received or not: copy not those who have freedom from anxiety, but cherish fear.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And when the Exorcism has been done, until the others who are being exorcised have come, let men be with men, and women with women.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For now I need the example of Noah’s ark: in which were Noah and his sons, and his wife and his sons’ wives.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For though the ark was one, and the door was shut, yet had things been suitably arranged.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: If the Church is shut, and you are all inside, yet let there be a separation, men with men, and women with women: lest the pretext of salvation become an occasion of destruction.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Even if there be a fair pretext for sitting near each other, let passions be put away.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Further, let the men when sitting have a useful book; and let one read, and another listen: and if there be no book, let one pray, and another speak something useful.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And again let the party of young women sit together in like manner, either singing or reading quietly, so that their lips speak, but others’ ears catch not the sound: for I suffer not a woman to speak in the Church.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And let the married woman also follow the same example, and pray; and let her lips move, but her voice be unheard, that a Samuel may come, and thy barren soul give birth to the salvation of “God who hath heard thy prayer;” for this is the interpretation of the name Samuel.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: I shall observe each man’s earnestness, each woman’s reverence.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Let your mind be refined as by fire unto reverence; let your soul be forged as metal: let the stubbornness of unbelief be hammered out: let the superfluous scales of the iron drop off, and what is pure remain; let the rust of the iron be rubbed off, and the true metal remain.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: May God sometime shew you that night, the darkness which shines like the day, concerning which it is said, The darkness shall not be hidden from thee, and the night shall shine as the day.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Then may the gate of Paradise be opened to every man and every woman among you.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Then may you enjoy the Christ-bearing waters in their fragrance.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Then may you receive the name of Christ, and the power of things divine.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Even now, I beseech you, lift up the eye of the mind: even now imagine the choirs of Angels, and God the Lord of all there sitting, and His Only-begotten Son sitting with Him on His right hand, and the Spirit present with them; and Thrones and Dominions doing service, and every man of you and every woman receiving salvation.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Even now let your ears ring, as it were, with that glorious sound, when over your salvation the angels shall chant, Blessed are they whose iniquities are forgiven, and whose sins are covered: when like stars of the Church you shall enter in, bright in the body and radiant in the soul.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Great is the Baptism that lies before you: a ransom to captives; a remission of offences; a death of sin; a new-birth of the soul; a garment of light; a holy indissoluble seal; a chariot to heaven; the delight of Paradise; a welcome into the kingdom; the gift of adoption!

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But there is a serpent by the wayside watching those who pass by: beware lest he bite thee with unbelief.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He sees so many receiving salvation, and is seeking whom he may devour.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou art coming in unto the Father of Spirits, but thou art going past that serpent.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: How then mayest thou pass him?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Have thy feet shod with the preparation of the gospel of peace; that even if he bite, he may not hurt thee.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Have faith in-dwelling, stedfast hope, a strong sandal, that thou mayest pass the enemy, and enter the presence of thy Lord.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Prepare thine own heart for reception of doctrine, for fellowship in holy mysteries.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Pray more frequently, that God may make thee worthy of the heavenly and immortal mysteries.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Cease not day nor night: but when sleep is banished from thine eyes, then let thy mind be free for prayer.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And if thou find any shameful thought rise up in thy mind, turn to meditation upon Judgment to remind thee of Salvation.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Give thy mind wholly to study, that it may forget base things.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: If thou find any one saying to thee, Art thou then going in, to descend into the water?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Has the city just now no baths? take notice that it is the dragon of the sea who is laying these plots against thee.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Attend not to the lips of the talker, but to God who worketh in thee.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Guard thine own soul, that thou be not ensnared, to the end that abiding in hope thou mayest become an heir of everlasting salvation.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: We for our part as men charge and teach you thus: but make not ye our building hay and stubble and chaff, lest we suffer loss, from our work being burnt up: but make ye our work gold, and silver, and precious stones!

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For it lies in me to speak, but in thee to set thy mind upon it, and in God to make perfect.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Let us nerve our minds, and brace up our souls, and prepare our hearts.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The race is for our soul: our hope is of things eternal: and God, who knoweth your hearts, and observeth who is sincere, and who a hypocrite, is able both to guard the sincere, and to give faith to the hypocrite: for even to the unbeliever, if only he give his heart, God is able to give faith.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: So may He blot out the handwriting that is against you, and grant you forgiveness of your former trespasses; may He plant you into His Church, and enlist you in His own service, and put on you the armour of righteousness: may He fill you with the heavenly things of the New Covenant, and give you the seal of the Holy Spirit indelible throughout all ages, in Christ Jesus Our Lord: to whom be the glory for ever and ever!

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: These Catechetical Lectures for those who are to be enlightened thou mayest lend to candidates for Baptism, and to believers who are already baptized, to read, but give not at all, neither to Catechumens, nor to any others who are not Christians, as thou shalt answer to the Lord.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And if thou make a copy, write this in the beginning, as in the sight of the Lord.
